title: Ripe Peruvian Cheddar Ceviche
description: A tangy, creamy ceviche that marries sharp mature cheddar with citrus and fresh herbs, perfect for a quick post-workout lunch.

ingredients.name: fresh white fish fillets
ingredients.notes: e.g., sea bass, firm and flaky

instructions: Dice the fresh white fish fillets into 1 cm cubes and place them in a glass bowl.
Add the lemon and lime juice, sea salt, and cracked pepper to the fish, stirring gently to coat evenly.
Let the fish marinate in the citrus mixture for 10 minutes in the refrigerator, turning once.
While the fish marinates, grate the mature cheddar cheese into a medium bowl.
After the marination period, drain the fish and combine it with the grated cheddar and thinly sliced red onion in the bowl.
Stir in the diced aji amarillo, chopped cilantro, and parsley until well mixed.
Gently fold in the rendered chicken fat to melt into the mixture, creating a silky texture.
Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt or pepper if desired.
Transfer the ceviche to a talavera plate, smoothing the surface with the back of a spoon.
Garnish with a few extra cilantro leaves and a thin slice of red onion on top.
Serve immediately as a refreshing post-workout lunch, chilled if preferred.
